name: Invasive mechanical ventilation
description: Patient on invasive mechanical ventilation (via endotracheal or tracheostomy tube); the act only — ventilation modes are separate.

en.name: Invasive mechanical ventilation
en.shortDescription: Patient on invasive mechanical ventilation (via endotracheal or tracheostomy tube); the act only — ventilation modes are separate.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Invasive mechanical ventilation (IMV) is breathing support delivered by a ventilator through an artificial airway that enters the trachea directly — an endotracheal tube or a tracheostomy tube. "Invasive" refers to that airway (a tube inside the trachea), as opposed to non-invasive ventilation, which is delivered through a mask or similar interface over the face. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating that the patient was on invasive ventilation; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care, anaesthesia, and emergency settings.

## Included Concepts

This set captures the act of invasive mechanical ventilation itself, as a single concept:

- *37158404 / 1258985005 — Invasive mechanical ventilation (SNOMED)*: the patient was mechanically ventilated through an endotracheal or tracheostomy tube.

The **ventilation mode** used (e.g. SIMV, assist-control, APRV, NAVA, high-frequency) is a separate dimension — modes apply to invasive and non-invasive ventilation alike — and is captured in the dedicated ventilation-mode concept sets, not here.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a record stating the patient was on invasive ventilation (via endotracheal or tracheostomy tube) to *37158404 / 1258985005 — Invasive mechanical ventilation*.
- **Mode is separate**: do not map a specific ventilation mode (SIMV, assist-control, pressure-control, volume-control, APRV, NAVA, high-frequency jet, etc.) to this set — those belong to the ventilation-mode concept sets. A patient can be on invasive ventilation *and* a specific mode at the same time; record each in its own set.
- **Invasive vs non-invasive**: ventilation delivered through a mask/interface (CPAP, BiPAP, non-invasive positive-pressure ventilation) is *not* invasive and belongs to the Non-invasive ventilation concept set.
- **The airway device itself** (the endotracheal or tracheostomy tube) and the act of placing it (intubation) are captured in their own concept sets; this set is only the ventilation act.

fr.name: Ventilation mécanique invasive
fr.shortDescription: Patient sous ventilation mécanique invasive (par sonde d’intubation ou de trachéotomie) ; le geste seul — les modes ventilatoires sont à part.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Reduce to the invasive mechanical ventilation act only (37158404). Removed ~18 ventilation-mode concepts (SIMV/ACV/APRV/NAVA/HFJV + variants — to be relocated to dedicated Ventilation mode sets), the non-invasive BiPAP titration (40491848, to NIV set 307), and the LOINC answer value (42530690). Added clinical description.
